sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Getafe are struggling, winless in their last 8 matches, with 3 draws and 2 losses in their last 5 games, conceding in every match. Celta Vigo, in contrast, are in better shape, securing 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss in their last 5 matches, showing offensive consistency by scoring in each game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, both teams have a win each, with Getafe winning 2-0 away at Celta Vigo in August 2025, and Celta Vigo clinching a 2-1 victory at Getafe in May 2025. Matches between these sides tend to be competitive, often with low-scoring outcomes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Celta Vigo, sitting 7th with 32 points, have a stronger attacking output with 29 goals scored compared to Getafe’s 16 (19th in the league). Getafe’s defense has conceded 27 goals, matching Celta’s tally, but their lack of possession (41.9%, 19th) and poor chance creation (27 big chances, 20th) highlight their struggles.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Celta Vigo, Borja Iglesias (8 goals) and Iago Aspas (2 goals, 3 assists) are threats, with Aspas historically performing well against Getafe (7.76 average rating). Getafe will rely on Luis Milla’s creativity (7 assists, 2nd in the league) to unlock Celta’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo’s superior form, league standing, and attacking edge, they are favorites to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 51% away win probability. Getafe’s home struggles and defensive vulnerabilities could be exploited by Celta’s consistent scoring ability.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Overall first half summary
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Getafe and Celta Vigo ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock at the Coliseum Alfonso Pérez. Getafe dominated much of the play, holding a slight edge in ball possession and showing more intent in the final third. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, struggled to create meaningful opportunities and were largely on the back foot for extended periods. Both teams picked up a yellow card each, indicating a competitive but disciplined encounter so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Getafe's dominance in attack
halftimeSections.text: Getafe were clearly the more proactive side in the first half, consistently pushing into the opposition's final third and generating a higher number of entries into dangerous areas. They managed a couple of efforts on target, showing intent to test the Celta Vigo defense. Their ability to win aerial duels at a high rate also gave them an edge in set-piece situations and long-ball plays. Players like Luis Milla stood out with creative contributions, while forwards such as Martín Satriano and Luis Vázquez showed glimpses of threat in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Celta Vigo's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo found themselves defending for much of the first half, with limited forays into Getafe's penalty area. They failed to register a single shot on target, highlighting their struggles to create in the final third. However, their defense held firm under pressure, with players like Marcos Alonso putting in a strong shift with multiple clearances. Despite being outplayed in aerial battles, they managed to keep the scoreline level, relying on a compact shape to frustrate Getafe's attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The early minutes saw Celta Vigo start with some initiative, pressing forward and looking to impose themselves. However, the momentum quickly shifted to Getafe, who took control for the majority of the half with sustained attacking spells, especially in the middle and latter stages. Celta Vigo had a brief resurgence around the midway point, stringing together a few promising moves, but they couldn't maintain the pressure. Getafe finished the half strongly, consistently building play in the opposition half and looking the more likely to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Getafe, the focus in the second half will be on converting their territorial advantage into clear-cut chances, as they've lacked the final touch despite their dominance. Improving the accuracy and decisiveness in front of goal will be crucial. Celta Vigo, meanwhile, need to find a way to transition more effectively into attack, as they've been too passive in possession. Bringing more energy and creativity in the final third will be essential if they are to turn the tide in this match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Given the pattern of play in the first half, Getafe are likely to continue their aggressive approach, pushing for a breakthrough with their home advantage. Celta Vigo will probably aim to tighten up defensively while looking for counter-attacking opportunities to exploit any gaps left by Getafe's forward momentum. The introduction of fresh legs through substitutions could play a pivotal role, especially for Celta, who may need a spark to reignite their attack. A goal seems more likely for the hosts if they can maintain their intensity.
